39 renewal is for a lower interest rate; or
40 (2) indebtedness that is approved in a local public question or
41 referendum under IC 6-1.1-20 or any other law.
42 (c) Subject to subsection (a) (before January 1, 2024) and
EH 1210—LS 6805/DI 134

106
1 subsection (b) (after December 31, 2023), a school corporation is
2 eligible to allocate credits proportionately under this section for 2019,
3 2020, 2021, 2022, 2023, 2024, 2025, or 2026 if the school corporation's
4 percentage computed under this subsection is at least ten percent (10%)
5 for its operations fund levy as certified by the department of local
6 government finance. A school corporation shall compute its percentage
7 under this subsection as determined under the following formula:
8 STEP ONE: Determine the amount of credits granted under this
9 chapter against the school corporation's levy for the school
10 corporation's operations fund.
11 STEP TWO: Determine the amount of the school corporation's
12 levy that is attributable to new debt incurred after June 30, 2019,
13 but is not attributable to the debt service levy described in
14 subsection (a)(1)(B) (before January 1, 2024) or subsection (b)(2)
15 (after December 31, 2023).
16 STEP THREE: Determine the result of the school corporation's
17 total levy minus any referendum levy.
18 STEP FOUR: Subtract the STEP TWO amount from the STEP
19 THREE amount.
20 STEP FIVE: Divide the STEP FOUR amount by the STEP
21 THREE amount expressed as a percentage.
22 STEP SIX: Multiply the STEP ONE amount by the STEP FIVE
23 percentage.
24 STEP SEVEN: Determine the school corporation's levy for the
25 school corporation's operations fund.
26 STEP EIGHT: Divide the STEP SIX amount by the STEP SEVEN
27 amount expressed as a percentage.
28 The computation must be made by taking into account the requirements
29 of section 9.8 of this chapter regarding protected taxes and the impact
30 of credits granted under this chapter on the revenue to be distributed to
31 the school corporation's operations fund for the particular year.
32 (d) A school corporation that desires to be an eligible school
33 corporation under this section must, before May 1 of the year for which
34 it wants a determination, submit a written request for a certification by
35 the department of local government finance that the computation of the
36 school corporation's percentage under subsection (c) is correct. The
37 department of local government finance shall, not later than June 1 of
38 that year, determine whether the percentage computed by the school
39 corporation under subsection (c) is accurate and certify whether the
40 school corporation is eligible under this section.
41 (e) For a school corporation that is certified as eligible under this
42 section, the school corporation may allocate the effect of the credits
EH 1210—LS 6805/DI 134

107
1 granted under this chapter and IC 6-1.1-49 proportionately among all
2 the school corporation's property tax funds that are not exempt under
3 section 7.5(b) or 7.5(c) of this chapter, based on the levy for each fund
4 and without taking into account the requirements of section 9.8 of this
5 chapter regarding protected taxes as determined under the following
6 formula:
7 STEP ONE: Determine the product of:
8 (A) the percentage determined under STEP EIGHT of
9 subsection (c); multiplied by
10 (B) five (5).
11 STEP TWO: Determine the lesser of the STEP ONE percentage
12 or one hundred percent (100%).
13 STEP THREE: Determine the product of:
14 (A) the amount determined under STEP SIX of subsection (c);
15 multiplied by
16 (B) the STEP TWO percentage.
17 The school corporation may allocate the amount of credits determined
18 under STEP THREE proportionately under this section. The
19 department of local government finance shall include in its certification
20 of an eligible school corporation under subsection (d) the amount of
21 credits that the school corporation may allocate proportionately as
22 determined under this subsection.
23 (f) This section expires January 1, 2027.

11 SECTION 98. IC 6-1.1-51.3-1, AS ADDED BY P.L.68-2025,
12 SECTION 84, IS AMENDED TO READ AS FOLLOWS [EFFECTIVE
13 JANUARY 1, 2026 (RETROACTIVE)]: Sec. 1. (a) An individual is
14 entitled to a credit against local property taxes imposed on the
15 individual's real property, or mobile home or manufactured home
16 within the county, if:
17 (1) the individual is at least sixty-five (65) years of age on or
18 before December 31 of the calendar year preceding the year in
19 which the credit is claimed;
20 (2) the individual has owned the real property, mobile home, or
21 manufactured home for at least one (1) year before claiming the
22 credit; or the individual has been buying the real property, mobile
23 home, or manufactured home under a contract that provides that
24 the individual is to pay the property taxes on the real property,
25 mobile home, or manufactured home for at least one (1) year
26 before claiming the credit, and the contract or a memorandum of
27 the contract is recorded in the county recorder's office;
28 (3) the individual:
29 (A) owns the real property, mobile home, or manufactured
30 home; or
31 (B) is buying the real property, mobile home, or manufactured
32 home under contract;
33 on the date the credit is claimed; and
34 (4) the:
35 (A) individual had, in the case of an individual who filed a
36 single return, adjusted gross income (as defined in Section 62
37 of the Internal Revenue Code) not exceeding sixty thousand
38 dollars ($60,000);
39 (B) individual had, in the case of an individual who filed a
40 joint income tax return with the individual's spouse, combined
41 adjusted gross income (as defined in Section 62 of the Internal
42 Revenue Code) not exceeding seventy thousand dollars
EH 1210—LS 6805/DI 134

124
1 ($70,000); or
2 (C) combined adjusted gross income (as defined in Section 62
3 of the Internal Revenue Code) of the individual and all other
4 individuals with whom:
5 (i) the individual shares ownership; or
6 (ii) the individual is purchasing the property under a
7 contract;
8 as joint tenants or tenants in common did not exceed seventy
9 thousand dollars ($70,000);
10 for the calendar year preceding by two (2) years the calendar year
11 in which the property taxes are first due and payable; and
12 (5) the individual resides on the real property, mobile home,
13 or manufactured home.
14 (b) The amount of the credit is equal to one hundred fifty dollars
15 ($150).
16 (c) An individual may not be denied the credit provided under this
17 section because the individual is absent from the real property, mobile
18 home, or manufactured home while in a nursing home or hospital.
19 (d) For purposes of this section, if real property, a mobile home, or
20 a manufactured home is owned by:
21 (1) tenants by the entirety;
22 (2) joint tenants; or
23 (3) tenants in common;
24 only one (1) credit may be allowed. However, the age requirement is
25 satisfied if any one (1) of the tenants is at least sixty-five (65) years of
26 age.
27 (e) A surviving spouse is entitled to the credit provided by this
28 section if:
29 (1) the surviving spouse is at least sixty (60) years of age on or
30 before December 31 of the calendar year preceding the year in
31 which the credit is claimed;
32 (2) the surviving spouse's deceased husband or wife was at least
33 sixty-five (65) years of age at the time of a death; and
34 (3) the surviving spouse has not remarried.
35 (f) An individual who has sold real property to another person under
36 a contract that provides that the contract buyer is to pay the property
37 taxes on the real property may not claim the credit provided under this
38 section against that real property.
39 (g) If individuals share ownership or are purchasing the property
40 under a contract as joint tenants or tenants in common and all of the
41 tenants are not at least sixty-five (65) years of age, the credit allowed
42 under this section shall be reduced by an amount equal to the credit
EH 1210—LS 6805/DI 134

125
1 multiplied by a fraction. The numerator of the fraction is the number of
2 tenants who are not at least sixty-five (65) years of age, and the
3 denominator is the total number of tenants.
4 (h) An individual wishing to claim a credit under this section must
5 file a statement, on forms prescribed by the department of local
6 government finance, with the county auditor and provide
7 documentation necessary to substantiate the individual's eligibility for
8 the credit. The statement must be completed and dated on or before
9 January 15 of the calendar year in which the property taxes are first due
10 and payable. The statement may be filed in person or by mail. If
11 mailed, the mailing must be postmarked on or before the last day for
12 filing. An individual who remains eligible for the credit in the
13 following year is not required to file a statement to apply for the credit
14 in the following year. However, an individual who receives a credit
15 under this section in a particular year and who becomes ineligible for
16 the credit in the following year shall notify the auditor of the county in
17 which the homestead is located of the individual's ineligibility not later
18 than sixty (60) days after the individual becomes ineligible.
19 SECTION 99. IC 6-1.1-51.3-5 IS ADDED TO THE INDIANA
20 CODE AS A NEW SECTION TO READ AS FOLLOWS
21 [EFFECTIVE JANUARY 1, 2026 (RETROACTIVE)]: Sec. 5. (a) An
22 individual is entitled to a credit against local property taxes
23 imposed on the individual's real property, or mobile home or
24 manufactured home within the county, if:
25 (1) the individual served in the military or naval forces of the
26 United States for at least ninety (90) days;
27 (2) the individual received an honorable discharge;
28 (3) the individual is at least sixty-two (62) years of age and has
29 a disability of at least ten percent (10%);
30 (4) the individual's disability is evidenced by:
31 (A) a pension certificate or an award of compensation
32 issued by the United States Department of Veterans
33 Affairs; or
34 (B) a certificate of eligibility issued to the individual by the
35 Indiana department of veterans' affairs after the Indiana
36 department of veterans' affairs has determined that the
37 individual's disability qualifies the individual to receive a
38 credit under this section; and
39 (5) the individual:
40 (A) owns the real property, mobile home, or manufactured
41 home; or
42 (B) is buying the real property, mobile home, or
EH 1210—LS 6805/DI 134

126
1 manufactured home under contract;
2 on the date the credit is claimed, and in the case of clause (B),
3 the contract or a memorandum of the contract is recorded in
4 the county recorder's office.
5 (b) The amount of the credit is equal to two hundred fifty
6 dollars ($250).
7 (c) The surviving spouse of an individual may receive the credit
8 provided by this section if:
9 (1) the individual satisfied the requirements of subsection
10 (a)(1) through (a)(4) at the time of death; or
11 (2) the individual:
12 (A) was killed in action;
13 (B) died while serving on active duty in the military or
14 naval forces of the United States; or
15 (C) died while performing inactive duty training in the
16 military or naval forces of the United States;
17 and the surviving spouse satisfies the requirement of subsection
18 (a)(5) at the time the credit is claimed. The surviving spouse is
19 entitled to the credit regardless of whether the property for which
20 the credit is claimed was owned by the deceased veteran or the
21 surviving spouse before the deceased veteran's death.
22 (d) An individual who receives the credit provided by this
23 section may receive any other property tax credit that the
24 individual is entitled to by law.
25 (e) An individual who has sold real property or a mobile home
26 or manufactured home to another person under a contract that
27 provides that the contract buyer is to pay the property taxes on the
28 real property, mobile home, or manufactured home may not claim
29 the credit provided under this section against that real property,
30 mobile home, or manufactured home.
31 (f) An individual wishing to claim a credit under this section
32 must file a statement, on forms prescribed by the department of
33 local government finance, with the county auditor and provide
34 documentation necessary to substantiate the individual's eligibility
35 for the credit. The statement must be completed and dated on or
36 before January 15 of the calendar year in which the property taxes
37 are first due and payable. The statement may be filed in person or
38 by mail. If mailed, the mailing must be postmarked on or before
39 the last day for filing. An individual who remains eligible for the
40 credit in the following year is not required to file a statement to
41 apply for the credit in the following year. However, an individual
42 who receives a credit under this section in a particular year and
EH 1210—LS 6805/DI 134

127
1 who becomes ineligible for the credit in the following year shall
2 notify the auditor of the county in which the homestead is located
3 of the individual's ineligibility not later than sixty (60) days after
4 the individual becomes ineligible.
5 (g) This subsection applies only for taxes assessed in 2026 and
6 due and payable in 2027. For all taxpayers that received a
7 deduction under IC 6-1.1-12-14 before the enactment of HEA
8 1210-2026, notwithstanding any other provision, the county
9 auditor shall not apply the deduction under IC 6-1.1-12-14 and
10 shall instead apply the local property tax credit under this section.
11 SECTION 100. IC 6-1.1-51.3-6 IS ADDED TO THE INDIANA

14 individual is entitled to a credit against local property taxes

18 United States during any of its wars;

21 disability of ten percent (10%) or more;
22 (4) the individual's disability is evidenced by:

27 Indiana department of veterans' affairs after the Indiana
28 department of veterans' affairs has determined that the
29 individual's disability qualifies the individual to receive a
30 credit under this section; and
31 (5) the individual:
32 (A) owns the real property, mobile home, or manufactured
33 home; or
34 (B) is buying the real property, mobile home, or
35 manufactured home under contract;
36 on the date the credit is claimed, and in the case of clause (B),
37 the contract or a memorandum of the contract is recorded in
38 the county recorder's office.
39 (b) The amount of the credit is equal to three hundred fifty
40 dollars ($350).
41 (c) The surviving spouse of an individual may receive the credit
42 provided by this section if the individual satisfied the requirements
EH 1210—LS 6805/DI 134

128
1 of subsection (a)(1) through (a)(4) at the time of death and the

7 "Manufactured home" means a manufactured home as that term is

9 on January 1, 1988. has the definition set forth in IC 9-13-2-96(a).

11 "Industrialized residential structure" means a structure that is both
12 an industrialized building system (as defined in IC 22-12-1-14) and a
13 one (1) or two (2) family private residence.

15 structures are exempt from the state gross retail tax to the extent that
16 the gross retail income from the sales is not attributable to the cost of
17 materials used in manufacturing the manufactured home or
18 industrialized residential structure.
19 (c) For purposes of this section, the part of the gross retail income
20 not attributable to the cost of materials used in manufacturing a
21 manufactured home or an industrialized residential structure is
22 thirty-five percent (35%) of the gross retail income derived from the
23 sale of the manufactured home or industrialized residential structure.
24 (d) The gross retail income derived from the sale of a preowned
25 manufactured home is exempt from the state gross retail tax.

13 changes a distribution or allocation of taxes takes effect as follows:
14 (1) An ordinance adopted on or before October 1 of a calendar
15 year shall take effect on January 1 of the calendar year that
16 immediately succeeds the year in which the ordinance is adopted.
17 (2) An ordinance adopted after October 1 of a calendar year shall
18 take effect on January 1 of the second succeeding calendar year
19 following the year the ordinance is adopted.
20 (e) An ordinance not described in subsections (b) through (d) takes
21 effect as provided under IC 36 for other ordinances of the
22 governmental entity adopting the ordinance.
23 (f) An ordinance described in section 7(e) or 7.5(e) of this chapter
24 that changes a tax rate or changes the allocation of revenue received
25 from a tax rate does not take effect as provided under this section if the
26 county adopting body fails to meet the required deadlines for notice
27 described in section 7(e) or 7.5(e) of this chapter. If an ordinance does
28 not take effect, the tax rate or allocation, as applicable, that is subject
29 to the proposed change in the ordinance shall be the lesser of the:
30 (1) applicable distribution schedule for the certified distribution
31 for the upcoming calendar year; or
32 (2) applicable distribution schedule for the certified distribution
33 for the current calendar year;
34 unless, or until, a subsequent ordinance is adopted and the required
35 deadlines for notice described in section 7(e) or 7.5(e) of this chapter
36 are met. This subsection expires January 1, 2025.

11 between January 1 and August 2 of a calendar year or October 2
12 and December 31 of a calendar year. If an adopting body adopts an
13 ordinance to impose a local income tax under:
14 (1) IC 6-3.6-6-2(b)(1) through IC 6-3.6-6-2(b)(4);
15 (2) IC 6-3.6-6-22; or
16 (3) IC 6-3.6-7;
17 that exceeds the applicable maximum tax rate or applicable
18 maximum aggregate tax rate allowable pursuant to IC 6-3.6-6-2,
19 IC 6-3.6-6-22, or IC 6-3.6-7, the department of local government
20 finance shall notify the adopting body and county fiscal officer or
21 municipal fiscal officer, as applicable, not later than thirty (30)
22 days after the adopting body submits the ordinance and
23 information required under IC 6-3.6-6-2 that one (1) or more tax
24 rates exceed the maximum allowable tax rate.
25 (c) This subsection applies only to an ordinance adopted
26 between January 1 and August 2 of a calendar year or October 2
27 and December 31 of a calendar year. Not later than thirty (30) days
28 after receiving a notification under subsection (b) from the
29 department of local government finance, the adopting body may
30 adopt an ordinance correcting the applicable tax rate or tax rates.
31 The following apply to an ordinance adopted under this subsection:
32 (1) Any statutory requirements for an ordinance that
33 otherwise apply to an ordinance adopted under this article to
34 impose a local income tax rate also apply to an ordinance
35 adopted under this subsection.
36 (2) If the tax rate or tax rates adopted in an ordinance
37 adopted under this subsection still exceed a maximum
38 allowable tax rate or maximum allowable aggregate tax rate,
39 the ordinance adopted under this subsection shall be
40 considered void and treated as if the adopting body did not
41 adopt any additional ordinance under this subsection.
42 (3) An ordinance adopted under this subsection has the same
EH 1210—LS 6805/DI 134

145
1 effective date as the initial ordinance described in subsection
2 (b).
3 (d) If an adopting body adopts an ordinance between August 3
4 and October 1 of a calendar year to impose a local income tax that
5 exceeds a maximum allowable tax rate or rates, fails to adopt an
6 ordinance correcting the applicable tax rate or tax rates under
7 subsection (c), or, the ordinance is described in subsection (c)(2),
8 the tax rate or rates will be reduced according to the following:
9 (1) If a tax rate or tax rates imposed pursuant to
10 IC 6-3.6-6-2(b)(1) through IC 6-3.6-6-2(b)(4), IC 6-3.6-6-22, or
11 IC 6-3.6-7 exceed the maximum allowable rate specified in
12 IC 6-3.6-6-2(b)(1) through IC 6-3.6-6-2(b)(4), IC 6-3.6-6-22, or
13 IC 6-3.6-7, the tax rate or tax rates that exceed the maximum
14 allowable rate shall be reduced to the maximum allowable
15 rate without further action by the adopting body.
16 (2) If the aggregate tax rates imposed pursuant to
17 IC 6-3.6-6-2(b)(1) through IC 6-3.6-6-2(b)(3) exceed the
18 maximum allowable aggregate rate in IC 6-3.6-6-2(c), the tax
19 rates shall be reduced without any further action by the
20 adopting body according to the following:
21 (A) Any portion of the aggregate tax rate that exceeds the
22 maximum allowable rate shall first be applied by reducing
23 the tax rate imposed under IC 6-3.6-6-2(b)(1), but may not
24 reduce the rate below the tax rate otherwise required
25 under this article.
26 (B) Any remaining portion of the aggregate tax rate that
27 exceeds the maximum allowable rate after the reduction in
28 clause (A) shall be applied to reduce the tax rates imposed
29 under IC 6-3.6-6-2(b)(2) and IC 6-3.6-6-2(b)(3) in
30 proportion to the total rates imposed under
31 IC 6-3.6-6-2(b)(2) and IC 6-3.6-6-2(b)(3).
32 (3) If the tax rate or rates exceed both the maximum allowable
33 rate specified in IC 6-3.6-6-2(b)(1) through IC 6-3.6-6-2(b)(3)
34 and the maximum allowable aggregate tax rate in
35 IC 6-3.6-6-2(c), the tax rates shall first be reduced in the
36 manner set forth in subdivision (1) before application of the
37 reduction manner set forth in subdivision (2).
38 (4) Any tax rate reduction under this subsection has the same
39 effective date as the initial ordinance described in subsection
40 (b).

8 this chapter shall be treated as follows:

27 (before its repeal) is in effect in a county, to make a

29 generated by the rate imposed under section 2.9 of this
30 chapter.

32 (before its expiration) is in effect in a county, to make a

1 to a school corporation or civil taxing unit shall be determined

22 this subdivision to the department of local government finance.
23 An ordinance adopted by a city or town under this subdivision is
24 not revocable and shall not expire following the next federal
25 decennial census. expire December 31, 2032.

41 distributions, if necessary, so that the apportionment and distributions

1 However, for purposes of determining distributions under this section

23 that would otherwise be available for distribution to taxing
24 units in the taxing district; multiplied by
25 (ii) the clause (C) amount.

37 districts in the county; divided by
38 (ii) the aggregate tax rate imposed in the taxing district for

41 (C) Divide the clause (B) amount by three (3).
42 (D) Determine the result of:

177
1 (i) the amount of excise taxes allocated to the taxing district
2 that would otherwise be available for distribution to taxing

4 (D) amount for the same taxing district; multiplied by
5 (ii) the clause (C) amount.
6 (E) Determine the sum of the clause (D) amounts for all taxing
7 districts in the county.

9 (A) Separately for 2006, 2007, and 2008 for each taxing
10 district in the county, determine the result of:

12 district plus the tax rate imposed by the school corporation
13 for the school corporation's special education preschool fund
14 in the district; divided by
15 (ii) the aggregate tax rate imposed in the taxing district for

23 (D) amount for the same taxing district; multiplied by
24 (ii) the clause (C) amount.

180
1 (c) An aircraft owner, who sells an aircraft on which the owner has
2 paid the tax imposed under this chapter, is entitled to a credit for the
3 tax paid. The credit equals excise tax paid on the aircraft that was sold,
4 times the lesser of:
5 (1) ninety percent (90%); or
6 (2) ten percent (10%) times the number of months remaining in
7 the registration year after the sale of the aircraft.
8 The credit may only be used to reduce the tax imposed under this
9 chapter on another aircraft purchased by that owner during the
10 registration year in which the credit accrues. A person may not receive
11 a refund for a credit under this subsection.
12 (d) A person who is entitled to a property tax deduction under
13 IC 6-1.1-12-13 (before its expiration) or IC 6-1.1-12-14 is entitled to
14 a credit against the tax imposed on the person's aircraft under this
15 chapter. The credit equals the amount of the property tax deduction to
16 which the person is entitled under IC 6-1.1-12-13 (before its
17 expiration) and IC 6-1.1-12-14 minus the amount of that deduction
18 used to offset the person's property taxes or vehicle excise taxes, times
19 seven hundredths (.07). The credit may not exceed the amount of the
20 tax due under this chapter. The county auditor shall, upon the person's
21 request, furnish a certified statement showing the credit allowable
22 under this subsection. The department may not allow a credit under this
23 subsection until the auditor's statement has been filed in the
24 department's office.

39 districts in the county.
40 STEP THREE: Determine the sum of the STEP ONE and STEP
41 TWO amounts for the county.
42 If the boundaries of a taxing district change after the years for which a
EH 1210—LS 6805/DI 134

185
1 ratio is calculated under STEP ONE or STEP TWO, the state
2 comptroller shall establish a ratio for the new taxing district that
3 reflects the tax rates imposed in the predecessor taxing districts. If a
4 new taxing district is established after the years for which a ratio is
5 calculated under STEP ONE, STEP TWO, or STEP THREE, the state
6 comptroller shall establish a ratio for the new taxing district and adjust
7 the ratio for other taxing districts in the county.
8 (c) The taxing unit may deposit distributions received under this
9 chapter in any fund maintained by the taxing unit, and the
10 distributions may be used for any purpose allowed by law.

36 begin on the date a tax is levied under section 6(a) of this chapter. The

38 end on December 31 of the year in which the tax is levied, and the
39 initial terms of the members appointed by the county council and by the
40 mayor of the largest city end on December 31 of the immediately
41 following year. All terms of office after the initial terms begin on
42 January 1 and end on December 31. After the initial terms, members of
EH 1210—LS 6805/DI 134

197
1 the commission appointed by the county council and by the mayor of
2 the largest city serve two (2) year terms, and members appointed by the
3 county commissioners serve one (1) year terms. A member whose term
4 expires may be reappointed to serve another term. If a vacancy occurs,
5 the commission, by majority vote shall, within thirty (30) days, appoint
6 a qualified person to serve the remainder of the term. If the commission
7 fails to appoint a person within thirty (30) days of the vacancy, the
8 original appointing official or body for that vacant position, by majority
9 vote, shall appoint a qualified person to serve the remainder of the
10 term.
11 (d) Members of the commission may not receive a salary. However,
12 commission members shall receive reimbursement for necessary
13 expenses, but only when the necessary expenses are incurred in the
14 performance of their respective duties.
15 (e) The commission must also contain a member appointed by
16 the city executive of each city within the county (other than the city
17 described in subsection (b)).

29 majority vote, shall appoint two (2) members of the commission, at
30 least one (1) of whom must be engaged in the hotel or motel business
31 within the county.
32 (c) All terms of office begin on January 1 and end on December 31.
33 Members of the commission appointed by the county council serve two
34 (2) year terms, and members appointed by the county commissioners

36 reappointed to serve another term. If a vacancy occurs, a qualified

38 for the remainder of the term.

5 SECTION 173. IC 6-9-15-2 IS AMENDED TO READ AS
6 FOLLOWS [EFFECTIVE JULY 1, 2026]: Sec. 2. (a) There is created
7 a seven (7) member board of managers (referred to as the "board" in
8 this chapter) plus any additional members appointed under
9 subsection (g), whose purpose is to promote the development and
10 growth of the convention activity, tourism and industry in the county.
11 (b) The board of county commissioners, by majority vote, shall
12 appoint three (3) members of the board, one (1) of whom must be
13 engaged in the lodging industry in the county, one (1) of whom must
14 be a county commissioner in the county, and one (1) of whom must be
15 a member of a chamber of commerce in the county. The city council of
16 the county's largest city according to the last preceding United States
17 decennial census shall, by majority vote, appoint three (3) members of
18 the board, one (1) of whom must be engaged in the lodging industry in
19 the county, one (1) of whom must be engaged in the travel industry in
20 the county, and one (1) of whom must be a member of the common
21 council of the county's largest city. The mayor of the city having the
22 largest population in the county according to the last preceding United
23 States decennial census shall appoint one (1) member who must be a
24 member of the county's business community.
25 (c) All terms of office begin on January 1 and end on December 31.
26 Members of the board appointed by the county commissioners serve
27 one (1) year terms, and the other members of the board serve two (2)
28 year terms. If a vacancy occurs, a qualified person shall be appointed
29 by the original appointing authority to serve for the remainder of the
30 term.
31 (d) A board member may be removed for cause by his appointing
32 authority.
33 (e) Members of the board may not receive a salary or reimbursement
34 for necessary expenses incurred in the performance of their respective
35 duties.
36 (f) Each board member, before entering his duties, shall take an oath
37 of office in the usual form, to be indorsed upon his certificate of
38 appointment, which shall be promptly filed with the clerk of the circuit
39 court of his county of residence.
40 (g) The board of managers must also contain a member
41 appointed by the city executive of each city within the county
42 (other than the city described in subsection (b)).

2 SECTION 175. IC 6-9-18-3, AS AMENDED BY THE
3 TECHNICAL CORRECTIONS BILL OF THE 2026 GENERAL
4 ASSEMBLY, IS AMENDED TO READ AS FOLLOWS [EFFECTIVE
5 JULY 1, 2026]: Sec. 3. (a) The fiscal body of a county may levy a tax
6 on every person engaged in the business of renting or furnishing, for
7 periods of less than thirty (30) days, any room or rooms, lodgings, or
8 accommodations in any:
9 (1) hotel;
10 (2) motel;
11 (3) boat motel;
12 (4) inn;
13 (5) college or university memorial union;
14 (6) college or university residence hall or dormitory; or
15 (7) tourist cabin;
16 located in the county.
17 (b) The tax does not apply to gross income received in a transaction
18 in which:
19 (1) a student rents lodgings in a college or university residence
20 hall while that student participates in a course of study for which
21 the student receives college credit from a college or university
22 located in the county; or
23 (2) a person rents a room, lodging, or accommodations for a
24 period of thirty (30) days or more.
25 (c) The tax may not exceed:
26 (1) the rate of five percent (5%) in a county other than a county
27 subject to subdivision (2), (3), or (4), or (5);
28 (2) after June 30, 2019, and except as provided in section 6.7 of
29 this chapter, the rate of eight percent (8%) in Howard County; or
30 (3) after June 30, 2021, the rate of nine percent (9%) in Daviess
31 County;
32 (4) subject to subsection (g), after June 30, 2026, the rate of
33 eight percent (8%) in DeKalb County; or
34 (5) subject to subsection (g), after June 30, 2026, the rate of
35 eight percent (8%) in Noble County.
36 The tax is imposed on the gross retail income derived from lodging
37 income only and is in addition to the state gross retail tax imposed
38 under IC 6-2.5.
39 (d) The county fiscal body may adopt an ordinance to require that
40 the tax shall be paid monthly to the county treasurer. If such an
41 ordinance is adopted, the tax shall be paid to the county treasurer not
42 more than twenty (20) days after the end of the month the tax is
EH 1210—LS 6805/DI 134

201
1 collected. If such an ordinance is not adopted, the tax shall be imposed,
2 paid, and collected in exactly the same manner as the state gross retail
3 tax is imposed, paid, and collected under IC 6-2.5.
4 (e) All of the provisions of IC 6-2.5 relating to rights, duties,
5 liabilities, procedures, penalties, definitions, exemptions, and
6 administration are applicable to the imposition and administration of
7 the tax imposed under this section except to the extent those provisions
8 are in conflict or inconsistent with the specific provisions of this
9 chapter or the requirements of the county treasurer. If the tax is paid to
10 the department of state revenue, the return to be filed for the payment
11 of the tax under this section may be either a separate return or may be
12 combined with the return filed for the payment of the state gross retail
13 tax as the department of state revenue may, by rule, determine.
14 (f) If the tax is paid to the department of state revenue, the amounts
15 received from the tax imposed under this section shall be paid monthly
16 by the treasurer of state to the county treasurer upon warrants issued by
17 the state comptroller.
18 (g) Beginning after December 31, 2048, a tax rate imposed in
19 DeKalb County under subsection (c)(4) and in Noble County under
20 subsection (c)(5) may not exceed five percent (5%). The portion of
21 a tax rate imposed in DeKalb County under subsection (c)(4) or in
22 Noble County under subsection (c)(5) that exceeds five percent
23 (5%) shall expire January 1, 2049.

30 into an agreement under IC 36-1-7.
31 (b) The county executive shall determine the number of members,
32 which must be an odd number, to be appointed to the commission.
33 However, the commission must also include any additional
34 members appointed under subsection (i). A simple majority of the
35 members must be:
36 (1) engaged in a convention, visitor, or tourism business; or
37 (2) involved in or promoting conventions, visitors, or tourism.
38 If available and willing to serve, at least two (2) of the members must
39 be engaged in the business of renting or furnishing rooms, lodging, or
40 accommodations (as described in section 3 of this chapter). Not more
41 than one (1) member may be affiliated with the same business entity.
42 Not more than a simple majority of the members may be affiliated with
EH 1210—LS 6805/DI 134

209
1 the same political party. Each member must reside in the county. The
2 county executive shall also determine who will make the appointments
3 to the commission, except that the executive of the largest municipality
4 in the county shall appoint a number of the members of the
5 commission, which number shall be in the same ratio to the total size
6 of the commission (rounded off to the nearest whole number) that the
7 population of the largest municipality bears to the total population of
8 the county.
9 (c) If a municipality other than the largest municipality in the county
10 collects fifty percent (50%) or more of the tax revenue collected under
11 this chapter during the three (3) month period following imposition of
12 the tax, the executive of the municipality shall appoint the same
13 number of members to the commission that the executive of the largest
14 municipality in the county appoints under subsection (b).
15 (d) Except as provided in subsection (c), all terms of office of
16 commission members begin on January 1. Initial appointments must be
17 for staggered terms, with subsequent appointments for two (2) year
18 terms. A member whose term expires may be reappointed to serve
19 another term. If a vacancy occurs, the appointing authority shall
20 appoint a qualified person to serve for the remainder of the term. If an
21 initial appointment is not made by February 1 or a vacancy is not filled
22 within thirty (30) days, the commission shall appoint a member by
23 majority vote.
24 (e) A member of the commission may be removed for cause by the
25 member's appointing authority.
26 (f) Members of the commission may not receive a salary. However,
27 commission members are entitled to reimbursement for necessary
28 expenses incurred in the performance of their respective duties.
29 (g) Each commission member, before entering the commission
30 member's duties, shall take an oath of office in the usual form, to be
31 endorsed upon the commission member's certificate of appointment
32 and promptly filed with the clerk of the circuit court of the county.
33 (h) The commission shall meet after January 1 each year for the
34 purpose of organization. It shall elect one (1) of its members president,
35 another vice president, another secretary, and another treasurer. The
36 members elected to those offices shall perform the duties pertaining to
37 the offices. The first officers chosen shall serve from the date of their
38 election until their successors are elected and qualified. A majority of
39 the commission constitutes a quorum, and the concurrence of a
40 majority of the commission is necessary to authorize any action.
41 (i) The commission must also include a member appointed by
42 the city executive of each city within the county (other than a city
EH 1210—LS 6805/DI 134

210
1 described in subsections (b) or (c)).

21 SECTION 185. IC 6-9-56-3, AS ADDED BY P.L.236-2023,
22 SECTION 121, IS AMENDED TO READ AS FOLLOWS
23 [EFFECTIVE JULY 1, 2026]: Sec. 3. (a) The fiscal body of the county
24 may impose a tax on every person engaged in the business of renting
25 or furnishing, for periods of less than thirty (30) days, any room or
26 rooms, lodgings, or accommodations in any:
27 (1) hotel;
28 (2) motel;
29 (3) boat motel;
30 (4) inn;
31 (5) college or university memorial union;
32 (6) college or university residence hall or dormitory; or
33 (7) tourist cabin;
34 located in the county.
35 (b) The tax does not apply to gross income received in a transaction
36 in which:
37 (1) a student rents lodgings in a college or university residence
38 hall while that student participates in a course of study for which
39 the student receives college credit from a college or university
40 located in the county; or
41 (2) a person rents a room, lodging, or accommodations for a
42 period of thirty (30) days or more.
EH 1210—LS 6805/DI 134

211
1 (c) The following apply to the tax rate imposed under this section:
2 (1) Before July 1, 2023, the tax may not exceed the rate of five
3 percent (5%) on the gross retail income derived from lodging
4 income only and is in addition to the state gross retail tax imposed
5 under IC 6-2.5.
6 (2) After June 30, 2023, and before January 1, 2049, the tax
7 may not exceed the rate of eight percent (8%) on the gross retail
8 income derived from lodging income only and is in addition to the
9 state gross retail tax imposed under IC 6-2.5.
10 (3) After December 31, 2048, a tax rate imposed under this
11 chapter may not exceed five percent (5%). The portion of the
12 tax rate imposed under subsection (2) that exceeds five
13 percent (5%) shall expire January 1, 2049.
14 (d) The county fiscal body may adopt an ordinance to require that
15 the tax shall be paid monthly to the county treasurer. If such an
16 ordinance is adopted, the tax shall be paid to the county treasurer not
17 more than twenty (20) days after the end of the month the tax is
18 collected. If such an ordinance is not adopted, the tax shall be imposed,
19 paid, and collected in exactly the same manner as the state gross retail
20 tax is imposed, paid, and collected under IC 6-2.5.
21 (e) All of the provisions of IC 6-2.5 relating to rights, duties,
22 liabilities, procedures, penalties, definitions, exemptions, and
23 administration are applicable to the imposition and administration of
24 the tax imposed under this section except to the extent those provisions
25 are in conflict or inconsistent with the specific provisions of this
26 chapter or the requirements of the county treasurer. If the tax is paid to
27 the department of state revenue, the return to be filed for the payment
28 of the tax under this section may be either a separate return or may be
29 combined with the return filed for the payment of the state gross retail
30 tax as the department of state revenue may, by rule, determine.

1 fund.

14 SECTION 189. IC 6-9-56-7.5 IS ADDED TO THE INDIANA
15 CODE AS A NEW SECTION TO READ AS FOLLOWS
16 [EFFECTIVE JULY 1, 2026]: Sec. 7.5. (a) For purposes of this
17 section, "fund" refers to the convention, visitor, tourism
18 promotion, and capital fund established under section 4(a)(2) of
19 this chapter.
20 (b) The county treasurer shall deposit in the fund the amount of
21 money received under section 3 of this chapter that is not more
22 than five percent (5%).
23 (c) Money in the fund shall be expended only as provided in
24 section 4(c) of this chapter.
25 (d) The commission may transfer money in the fund to any
26 Indiana nonprofit corporation for the purpose of promotion and
27 encouragement in the county of conventions, trade shows, visitors,
28 or special events. The commission may transfer money under this
29 subsection only after approving the transfer. The commission may
30 transfer money under this subsection on a monthly basis or at
31 another frequency as determined by the commission.
32 (e) The commission must approve any transfer of money from
33 the fund and may transfer money from the fund to support capital
34 projects in the county that promote long term tourism, convention,
35 or recreation projects proposed by any of the following:
36 (1) The county government.
37 (2) A separate body corporate and politic in Hamilton County.
38 (3) Any Indiana nonprofit corporation in Hamilton County.
39 The commission may transfer money under this subsection on a
40 monthly basis or at another frequency as determined by the
41 commission.
42 (f) The commission may also review and approve proposals
EH 1210—LS 6805/DI 134

215
1 submitted by applicants that seek money from the fund with the
2 purpose and view of enhancing or providing support for capital
3 projects that promote long term tourism, convention, or other
4 economic development related to recreation. Funding available
5 under this subsection shall be made available on an annual basis.
6 In determining whether to provide funding to a particular capital
7 project under this subsection, the commission may use the
8 following factors as a guide for capital project funding:
9 (1) The proposed capital project is believed to be economically
10 sound to the Hamilton County tourism, convention, or
11 recreation economy and is also believed to be beneficial to:
12 (A) the general population of Hamilton County; or
13 (B) a particular location in Hamilton County.
14 (2) The proposed capital project provides for reasonably
15 adequate public assembly, gathering, or entertainment space
16 and is integrally related to enhancing the tourism, convention,
17 or recreation opportunities in Hamilton County or a
18 particular location in Hamilton County.
19 (3) The commission makes a reasonable effort to assess
20 whether a proposed capital project aligns with the purpose of
21 the commission and has a direct, indirect, or supportive
22 relationship to the mission and promotional efforts of the
23 commission as established and funded by the fund.
24 Any remaining funds collected that are not awarded during an
25 application period revert to the fund and may be used for
26 distribution in a subsequent application period.
27 (g) An applicant that receives a grant of money from the fund
28 under subsection (f):
29 (1) must agree to provide to the commission proof of project
30 completion, including proof that the project was completed
31 through the use of the grant money; and
32 (2) may be subject to annual financial reporting and audit.

2 SECTION 191. IC 6-9-56-8.5 IS ADDED TO THE INDIANA
3 CODE AS A NEW SECTION TO READ AS FOLLOWS
4 [EFFECTIVE JULY 1, 2026]: Sec. 8.5. (a) The county treasurer shall
5 transfer the amount of money received under section 3(c)(2) of this
6 chapter that is generated by a rate that exceeds five percent (5%)
7 to the fiscal officer of each of the following cities with each city
8 receiving an equal twenty-five percent (25%) share of the total
9 amount collected:
10 (1) Noblesville.
11 (2) Carmel.
12 (3) Fishers.
13 (4) Westfield.
14 (b) The fiscal officer of each city under subsection (a) shall
15 establish a municipal tourism capital fund. The fiscal officer shall
16 deposit in the fund all money received by the city under this
17 section. The city fiscal body shall administer the fund. The city may
18 not establish a tourism board or similar entity for any purposes of
19 the fund and the city fiscal body shall have sole authority regarding
20 the use of money in the fund as set forth under subsection (c).
21 (c) Money in the fund may be used only for capital projects for
22 tourism related purposes as determined by the city fiscal body. The
23 city fiscal body may issue bonds, enter into leases, or incur other
24 obligations for the purposes of this subsection.
25 (d) Money transferred to a city under subsection (a) shall not be
26 used by the city for tourism marketing, tourism promotion, or
27 tourism planning purposes.

4 shall take an oath of office in the usual form, to be endorsed upon the
5 member's certificate of appointment and promptly filed with the clerk
6 of the circuit court of the county.
7 (g) The commission shall meet after January 1 each year for the
8 purpose of organization. It shall elect one (1) of its members president,
9 another vice president, another secretary, and another treasurer. The
10 members elected to those offices shall perform the duties pertaining to
11 the offices. The first officers chosen shall serve from the date of their
12 election until their successors are elected and qualified. A majority of
13 the commission constitutes a quorum, and the concurrence of a
14 majority of the commission is necessary to authorize any action.
15 (h) The commission must also include a member appointed by
16 the city executive of each city within the county.
17 SECTION 195. IC 6-9-76-7, AS ADDED BY P.L.230-2025,
18 SECTION 125, IS AMENDED TO READ AS FOLLOWS
19 [EFFECTIVE JULY 1, 2026]: Sec. 7. (a) The county executive shall
20 create a commission to promote the development and growth of the
21 convention, visitor, and tourism industry in the county. If two (2) or
22 more adjoining counties desire to establish a joint commission, the
23 counties shall enter into an agreement under IC 36-1-7.
24 (b) The county executive shall determine the number of members,
25 which must be an odd number, to be appointed to the commission. The
26 commission must also include any additional members appointed
27 under subsection (h). Each of the members must be:
28 (1) engaged in a convention, visitor, or tourism business; or
29 (2) involved in or promoting conventions, visitors, or tourism.
30 A member who is an owner or an executive level employee of a
31 convention, visitor, or tourism related business located in the county is
32 not required to reside in the county but must reside in Indiana. A
33 member who is not an owner or an executive level employee of a
34 convention, visitor, or tourism related business located in the county
35 must reside in the county. If available and willing to serve, at least two
36 (2) of the members must be engaged in the business of renting or
37 furnishing rooms, lodging, or accommodations (as described in section
38 3 of this chapter). The county executive shall also determine who will
39 make the appointments to the commission.
40 (c) All terms of office of commission members begin on January 1.
41 Initial appointments must be for staggered terms, with subsequent
42 appointments for three (3) year terms. A member whose term expires
EH 1210—LS 6805/DI 134

222
1 may be reappointed to serve another term. If a vacancy occurs, the
2 appointing authority shall appoint a qualified person to serve for the
3 remainder of the term. If an initial appointment is not made by
4 February 1 or a vacancy is not filled within thirty (30) days, the
5 commission shall appoint a member by majority vote.
6 (d) A member of the commission may be removed for cause by the
7 member's appointing authority.
8 (e) Members of the commission may not receive a salary. However,
9 commission members are entitled to reimbursement for necessary
10 expenses incurred in the performance of their respective duties.
11 (f) Each commission member, before entering the member's duties,
12 shall take an oath of office in the usual form, to be endorsed upon the
13 member's certificate of appointment and promptly filed with the clerk
14 of the circuit court of the county.
15 (g) The commission shall meet after January 1 each year for the
16 purpose of organization. It shall elect one (1) of its members president,
17 another vice president, another secretary, and another treasurer. The
18 members elected to those offices shall perform the duties pertaining to
19 the offices. The first officers chosen shall serve from the date of their
20 election until their successors are elected and qualified. A majority of
21 the commission constitutes a quorum, and the concurrence of a
22 majority of the commission is necessary to authorize any action.
23 (h) The commission must also include a member appointed by
24 the city executive of each city within the county.
25 SECTION 196. IC 6-9-78.1 IS ADDED TO THE INDIANA CODE
26 AS A NEW CHAPTER TO READ AS FOLLOWS [EFFECTIVE
27 JULY 1, 2026]:
28 Chapter 78.1. Lagro Food and Beverage Tax
29 Sec. 1. This chapter applies to the town of Lagro.
30 Sec. 2. The definitions in IC 6-9-12-1 apply throughout this
31 chapter.
32 Sec. 3. (a) The fiscal body of the town may adopt an ordinance
33 to impose an excise tax, known as the town food and beverage tax,
34 on transactions described in section 4 of this chapter. The fiscal
35 body of the town may adopt an ordinance under this subsection
36 only after the town fiscal body has previously:
37 (1) adopted a resolution in support of the proposed town food
38 and beverage tax; and
39 (2) held at least one (1) separate public hearing in which a
40 discussion of the proposed ordinance to impose the town food
41 and beverage tax is the only substantive issue on the agenda
42 for the public hearing.
EH 1210—LS 6805/DI 134

223
1 (b) If the town fiscal body adopts an ordinance under subsection
2 (a), the town fiscal body shall immediately send a certified copy of
3 the ordinance to the department of state revenue.
4 (c) If the town fiscal body adopts an ordinance under subsection
5 (a), the town food and beverage tax applies to transactions that
6 occur after the last day of the month following the month in which
7 the ordinance is adopted.
8 Sec. 4. (a) Except as provided in subsection (c), a tax imposed
9 under section 3 of this chapter applies to a transaction in which
10 food or beverage is furnished, prepared, or served:
11 (1) for consumption at a location or on equipment provided by
12 a retail merchant;
13 (2) in the town; and
14 (3) by a retail merchant for consideration.
15 (b) Transactions described in subsection (a)(1) include
16 transactions in which food or beverage is:
17 (1) served by a retail merchant off the merchant's premises;
18 (2) food sold in a heated state or heated by a retail merchant;
19 (3) made of two (2) or more food ingredients, mixed or
20 combined by a retail merchant for sale as a single item (other
21 than food that is only cut, repackaged, or pasteurized by the
22 seller, and eggs, fish, meat, poultry, and foods containing these
23 raw animal foods requiring cooking by the consumer as
24 recommended by the federal Food and Drug Administration
25 in chapter 3, subpart 3-401.11 of its Food Code so as to
26 prevent food borne illnesses); or
27 (4) food sold with eating utensils provided by a retail
28 merchant, including plates, knives, forks, spoons, glasses,
29 cups, napkins, or straws (for purposes of this subdivision, a
30 plate does not include a container or package used to
31 transport the food).
32 (c) The town food and beverage tax does not apply to the
33 furnishing, preparing, or serving of a food or beverage in a
34 transaction that is exempt, or to the extent the transaction is
35 exempt, from the state gross retail tax imposed by IC 6-2.5.
36 Sec. 5. The town food and beverage tax rate:
37 (1) must be imposed in an increment of twenty-five
38 hundredths percent (0.25%); and
39 (2) may not exceed one percent (1%);
40 of the gross retail income received by the merchant from the food
41 or beverage transaction described in section 4 of this chapter. For
42 purposes of this chapter, the gross retail income received by the
EH 1210—LS 6805/DI 134

224
1 retail merchant from a transaction does not include the amount of
2 tax imposed on the transaction under IC 6-2.5.
3 Sec. 6. A tax imposed under this chapter shall be imposed, paid,
4 and collected in the same manner that the state gross retail tax is
5 imposed, paid, and collected under IC 6-2.5. However, the return
6 to be filed with the payment of the tax imposed under this chapter
7 may be made on a separate return or may be combined with the
8 return filed for the payment of the state gross retail tax, as
9 prescribed by the department of state revenue.
10 Sec. 7. The amounts received from the tax imposed under this
11 chapter shall be paid monthly by the treasurer of state to the town
12 fiscal officer upon warrants issued by the state comptroller.
13 Sec. 8. (a) If a tax is imposed under section 3 of this chapter by
14 the town, the town fiscal officer shall establish a food and beverage
15 tax receipts fund.
16 (b) The town fiscal officer shall deposit in the fund all amounts
17 received under this chapter.
18 (c) Money earned from the investment of money in the fund
19 becomes a part of the fund.
20 Sec. 9. Money in the food and beverage tax receipts fund must
21 be used by the town only for the following purposes:
22 (1) For economic development purposes, including the pledge
23 of money under IC 5-1-14-4 for bonds, leases, or other
24 obligations for economic development purposes.
25 (2) For park and recreation purposes, including the purchase
26 of land for park and recreation purposes.
27 (3) The pledge of money under IC 5-1-14-4 for bonds, leases,
28 or other obligations incurred for a purpose described in
29 subdivision (2).
30 Sec. 10. With respect to obligations for which a pledge has been
31 made under section 9 of this chapter, the general assembly
32 covenants with the holders of the obligations that this chapter will
33 not be repealed or amended in a manner that will adversely affect
34 the imposition or collection of the tax imposed under this chapter
35 if the payment of any of the obligations is outstanding.
36 Sec. 11. (a) If the town imposes the tax authorized by this
37 chapter, the tax terminates on January 1, 2049.
38 (b) This chapter expires January 1, 2049.

35 beverage tax under this chapter. If the city of Huntington is the

38 prohibited from imposing a food and beverage tax under this
39 chapter.

12 SECTION 200. IC 8-22-3.5-9, AS AMENDED BY P.L.174-2022,
13 SECTION 50, IS AMENDED TO READ AS FOLLOWS [EFFECTIVE
14 JULY 1, 2026]: Sec. 9. (a) As used in this section, "base assessed
15 value" means, subject to subsection (k):
16 (1) the net assessed value of all the tangible property as finally
17 determined for the assessment date immediately preceding the
18 effective date of the allocation provision of the commission's
19 resolution adopted under section 5 or 9.5 of this chapter,
20 notwithstanding the date of the final action taken under section 6
21 of this chapter; plus
22 (2) to the extent it is not included in subdivision (1), the net
23 assessed value of property that is assessed as residential property
24 under the rules of the department of local government finance,
25 within the airport development zone, as finally determined for the
26 current assessment date.
27 However, subdivision (2) applies only to an airport development zone
28 established after June 30, 1997, and the portion of an airport
29 development zone established before June 30, 1997, that is added to an
30 existing airport development zone.
31 (b) A resolution adopted under section 5 of this chapter and
32 confirmed under section 6 of this chapter must include a provision with
33 respect to the allocation and distribution of property taxes for the
34 purposes and in the manner provided in this section.
35 (c) The allocation provision must:
36 (1) apply to the entire airport development zone; and
37 (2) require that any property tax on taxable tangible property
38 subsequently levied by or for the benefit of any public body
39 entitled to a distribution of property taxes in the airport
40 development zone be allocated and distributed as provided in
41 subsections (d) and (e).
42 (d) Except as otherwise provided in this section:
EH 1210—LS 6805/DI 134

233
1 (1) the proceeds of the taxes attributable to the lesser of:
2 (A) the assessed value of the tangible property for the
3 assessment date with respect to which the allocation and
4 distribution is made; or
5 (B) the base assessed value;
6 shall be allocated and, when collected, paid into the funds of the
7 respective taxing units; and
8 (2) the excess of the proceeds of the property taxes imposed for
9 the assessment date with respect to which the allocation and
10 distribution are made that are attributable to taxes imposed after
11 being approved by the voters in a referendum or local public
12 question conducted after April 30, 2010, not otherwise included
13 in subdivision (1) shall be allocated to and, when collected, paid
14 into the funds of the taxing unit for which the referendum or local
15 public question was conducted.
16 (e) All of the property tax proceeds in excess of those described in
17 subsection (d) shall be allocated to the eligible entity for the airport
18 development zone and, when collected, paid into special funds as
19 follows:
20 (1) The commission may determine that a portion of tax proceeds
21 shall be allocated to a training grant fund to be expended by the
22 commission without appropriation solely for the purpose of
23 reimbursing training expenses incurred by public or private
24 entities in the training of employees for the qualified airport
25 development project.
26 (2) The commission may determine that a portion of tax proceeds
27 shall be allocated to a debt service fund and dedicated to the
28 payment of principal and interest on revenue bonds or a loan
29 contract of the board of aviation commissioners or airport
30 authority for a qualified airport development project, to the
31 payment of leases for a qualified airport development project, or
32 to the payment of principal and interest on bonds issued by an
33 eligible entity to pay for qualified airport development projects in
34 the airport development zone or serving the airport development
35 zone.
36 (3) The commission may determine that a part of the tax proceeds
37 shall be allocated to a project fund and used to pay expenses
38 incurred by the commission for a qualified airport development
39 project that is in the airport development zone or is serving the
40 airport development zone.
41 (4) Except as provided in subsection (f), all remaining tax
42 proceeds after allocations are made under subdivisions (1), (2),
EH 1210—LS 6805/DI 134

234
1 and (3) shall be allocated to a project fund and dedicated to the
2 reimbursement of expenditures made by the commission for a
3 qualified airport development project that is in the airport
4 development zone or is serving the airport development zone.
5 (f) Before July 15 of each year, the commission shall do the
6 following:
7 (1) Determine the amount, if any, by which tax proceeds allocated
8 to the project fund in subsection (e)(3) in the following year will
9 exceed the amount necessary to satisfy amounts required under
10 subsection (e).
11 (2) Provide a written notice to the county auditor and the officers
12 who are authorized to fix budgets, tax rates, and tax levies under
13 IC 6-1.1-17-5 for each of the other taxing units that is wholly or
14 partly located within the allocation area. The notice must:
15 (A) state the amount, if any, of excess tax proceeds that the
16 commission has determined may be allocated to the respective
17 taxing units in the manner prescribed in subsection (d)(1); or
18 (B) state that the commission has determined that there are no
19 excess tax proceeds that may be allocated to the respective
20 taxing units in the manner prescribed in subsection (d)(1).
21 The county auditor shall allocate to the respective taxing units the
22 amount, if any, of excess tax proceeds determined by the
23 commission.
24 (g) When money in the debt service fund and in the project fund is
25 sufficient to pay all outstanding principal and interest (to the earliest
26 date on which the obligations can be redeemed) on revenue bonds
27 issued by the board of aviation commissioners or airport authority for
28 the financing of qualified airport development projects, all lease rentals
29 payable on leases of qualified airport development projects, and all
30 costs and expenditures associated with all qualified airport
31 development projects, money in the debt service fund and in the project
32 fund in excess of those amounts shall be paid to the respective taxing
33 units in the manner prescribed by subsection (d)(1).
34 (h) Property tax proceeds allocable to the debt service fund under
35 subsection (e)(2) must, subject to subsection (g), be irrevocably
36 pledged by the eligible entity for the purpose set forth in subsection
37 (e)(2).
38 (i) Notwithstanding any other law, each assessor shall, upon petition
39 of the commission, reassess the taxable tangible property situated upon
40 or in, or added to, the airport development zone effective on the next
41 assessment date after the petition.
42 (j) Notwithstanding any other law, the assessed value of all taxable

1 tangible property in the airport development zone, for purposes of tax

7 (2) the base assessed value.

21 (1) may run for a period not exceeding fifteen (15) years;
22 (2) may bear interest at any rate; and
23 (3) shall be sold for not less than par.
24 The township trustee, before issuing the warrants or bonds, shall place
25 a notice in accordance with IC 5-3-1-4 IC 5-3-1-1.5 in at least one (1)
26 appropriate publication announcing the sale of the bonds in at least one
27 (1) issue a week for three (3) weeks. The notice must comply with
28 IC 5-3-1 and must set forth the amount of bonds offered, the
29 denomination, the period to run, the rate of interest, and the date, place,
30 and time of selling. The township board shall attend the bond sale and
31 must concur in the sale before the bonds are sold.
32 (b) The board shall annually levy sufficient taxes each year to pay
33 at least one-fifteenth (1/15) of the warrants or bonds, including interest,
34 and the trustee shall apply the annual tax to the payment of the warrants
35 or bonds each year.
36 (c) A debt of the township may not be created except by the
37 township board in the manner specified in this section. The board may
38 bring an action in the name of the state against the bond of a trustee to
39 recover for the use of the township funds expended in the unauthorized
40 payment of a debt. The board may appropriate and the township trustee
41 shall pay from township funds a reasonable sum for attorney's fees for
42 this purpose.
EH 1210—LS 6805/DI 134

246
1 (d) If a taxpayer serves the board with a written demand that the
2 board bring an action as described in subsection (c), and after thirty
3 (30) days the board has not brought an action, a taxpayer may bring an
4 action to recover for the use of the township funds expended in the
5 unauthorized payment of a debt. An action brought under this
6 subsection shall be brought in the name of the state.

1 (A) Whatever is acquired upon the sale, lease, license,
2 exchange, or other disposition of collateral.
3 (B) Whatever is collected on, or distributed on account of,
4 collateral.
5 (C) Rights arising out of collateral.
6 (D) To the extent of the value of collateral, claims arising out
7 of the loss, nonconformity, or interference with the use of,
8 defects or infringement of rights in, or damage to, the
9 collateral.
10 (E) To the extent of the value of collateral and to the extent
11 payable to the debtor or the secured party, insurance payable
12 by reason of the loss or nonconformity of, defects or
13 infringement of rights in, or damage to, the collateral.
14 (65) "Promissory note" means an instrument that evidences a
15 promise to pay a monetary obligation, does not evidence an order
16 to pay, and does not contain an acknowledgment by a bank that
17 the bank has received for deposit a sum of money or funds.
18 (66) "Proposal" means a record signed by a secured party that
19 includes the terms on which the secured party is willing to accept
20 collateral in full or partial satisfaction of the obligation it secures
21 pursuant to IC 26-1-9.1-620, IC 26-1-9.1-621, and
22 IC 26-1-9.1-622.
23 (67) "Public-finance transaction" means a secured transaction in
24 connection with which:
25 (A) debt securities are issued;
26 (B) all or a portion of the securities issued have an initial
27 stated maturity of at least twenty (20) years; and
28 (C) the debtor, obligor, secured party, account debtor, or other
29 person obligated on collateral, assignor or assignee of a
30 secured obligation, or assignor or assignee of a security
31 interest is a state or a governmental unit of a state.
32 (68) "Public organic record" means a record that is available to
33 the public for inspection and is:
34 (A) a record consisting of the record initially filed with or
35 issued by a state or the United States to form or organize an
36 organization and any record filed with or issued by the state or
37 the United States which amends or restates the initial record;
38 (B) an organic record of a business trust consisting of the
39 record initially filed with a state and any record filed with the
40 state which amends or restates the initial record, if a statute of
41 the state governing business trusts requires that the record be
42 filed with the state; or
EH 1210—LS 6805/DI 134

259
1 (C) a record consisting of legislation enacted by the legislature
2 of a state or the Congress of the United States which forms or
3 organizes an organization, any record amending the
4 legislation, and any record filed with or issued by the state or
5 the United States which amends or restates the name of the
6 organization.
7 (69) "Pursuant to commitment", with respect to an advance made
8 or other value given by a secured party, means pursuant to the
9 secured party's obligation, whether or not a subsequent event of
10 default or other event not within the secured party's control has
11 relieved or may relieve the secured party from its obligation.
12 (70) "Record", except as used in "for record", "of record", "record
13 or legal title", and "record owner", means information that is
14 inscribed on a tangible medium or that is stored in an electronic
15 or other medium and is retrievable in perceivable form.
16 (71) "Registered organization" means an organization formed or
17 organized solely under the law of a single state or the United
18 States by the filing of a public organic record with, the issuance
19 of a public organic record by, or the enactment of legislation by
20 the state or the United States. The term includes a business trust
21 that is formed or organized under the law of a single state if a
22 statute of the state governing business trusts requires that the
23 business trust's organic record be filed with the state.
24 (72) "Secondary obligor" means an obligor to the extent that:
25 (A) the obligor's obligation is secondary; or
26 (B) the obligor has a right of recourse with respect to an
27 obligation secured by collateral against the debtor, another
28 obligor, or property of either.
29 (73) "Secured party" means:
30 (A) a person in whose favor a security interest is created or
31 provided for under a security agreement, whether or not any
32 obligation to be secured is outstanding;
33 (B) a person that holds an agricultural lien;
34 (C) a consignor;
35 (D) a person to which accounts, chattel paper, payment
36 intangibles, or promissory notes have been sold;
37 (E) a trustee, indenture trustee, agent, collateral agent, or other
38 representative in whose favor a security interest or agricultural
39 lien is created or provided for; or
40 (F) a person that holds a security interest arising under
41 IC 26-1-2-401, IC 26-1-2-505, IC 26-1-2-711(3),
42 IC 26-1-2.1-508(5), IC 26-1-4-210, or IC 26-1-5.1-118.

13 subdivision to the county assessor.

30 hearing, however, the staff (as defined in the zoning ordinance), if any,

32 opinions relating to the matter.

41 (1) the assessed value of the property as valued without regard to
42 this section; or
EH 1210—LS 6805/DI 134

283
1 (2) the base assessed value.
2 (g) If any part of the allocation area is located in an enterprise zone
3 created under IC 5-28-15, the unit that designated the allocation area
4 shall create funds as specified in this subsection. A unit that has
5 obligations, bonds, or leases payable from allocated tax proceeds under
6 subsection (b)(4) shall establish an allocation fund for the purposes
7 specified in subsection (b)(4) and a special zone fund. Such a unit
8 shall, until the end of the enterprise zone phase out period, deposit each
9 year in the special zone fund any amount in the allocation fund derived
10 from property tax proceeds in excess of those described in subsection
11 (b)(1), (b)(2), and (b)(3) from property located in the enterprise zone
12 that exceeds the amount sufficient for the purposes specified in
13 subsection (b)(4) for the year. The amount sufficient for purposes
14 specified in subsection (b)(4) for the year shall be determined based on
15 the pro rata portion of such current property tax proceeds from the part
16 of the enterprise zone that is within the allocation area as compared to
17 all such current property tax proceeds derived from the allocation area.
18 A unit that has no obligations, bonds, or leases payable from allocated
19 tax proceeds under subsection (b)(4) shall establish a special zone fund
20 and deposit all the property tax proceeds in excess of those described
21 in subsection (b)(1), (b)(2), and (b)(3) in the fund derived from
22 property tax proceeds in excess of those described in subsection (b)(1),
23 (b)(2), and (b)(3) from property located in the enterprise zone. The unit
24 that creates the special zone fund shall use the fund (based on the
25 recommendations of the urban enterprise association) for programs in
26 job training, job enrichment, and basic skill development that are
27 designed to benefit residents and employers in the enterprise zone or
28 other purposes specified in subsection (b)(4), except that where
29 reference is made in subsection (b)(4) to allocation area it shall refer
30 for purposes of payments from the special zone fund only to that part
31 of the allocation area that is also located in the enterprise zone. Those
32 programs shall reserve at least one-half (1/2) of their enrollment in any
33 session for residents of the enterprise zone.
34 (h) The state board of accounts and department of local government
35 finance shall make the rules and prescribe the forms and procedures
36 that they consider expedient for the implementation of this chapter.
37 After each reassessment in an area under a reassessment plan prepared
38 under IC 6-1.1-4-4.2, the department of local government finance

40 local government finance, adjust the base assessed value one (1) time
41 to neutralize any effect of the reassessment of the real property in the
42 area on the property tax proceeds allocated to the redevelopment

284
1 district under this section. After each annual adjustment under

5 neutralize any effect of the annual adjustment on the property tax

8 (1) may not include the effect of phasing in assessed value due to

27 (i) The allocation deadline referred to in subsection (b) is
28 determined in the following manner:
29 (1) The initial allocation deadline is December 31, 2011.
30 (2) Subject to subdivision (3), the initial allocation deadline and

32 increments of five (5) years, so that allocation deadlines
33 subsequent to the initial allocation deadline fall on December 31,
34 2016, and December 31 of each fifth year thereafter.
35 (3) At least one (1) year before the date of an allocation deadline
36 determined under subdivision (2), the general assembly may enact
37 a law that:

39 under subdivision (2); and
40 (B) specifically designates a particular date as the final
41 allocation deadline.

1 or an amendment to a declaratory resolution that contains an allocation

12 taxes for the purposes and in the manner provided in this section. A

14 the amendment of that resolution on or before the allocation deadline
15 determined under subsection (i) in accordance with the procedures
16 required for its original adoption. A declaratory resolution or
17 amendment that establishes an allocation provision must include a
18 specific finding of fact, supported by evidence, that the adoption of the
19 allocation provision will result in new property taxes in the area that
20 would not have been generated but for the adoption of the allocation
21 provision. For an allocation area established before July 1, 1995, the
22 expiration date of any allocation provisions for the allocation area is
23 June 30, 2025, or the last date of any obligations that are outstanding
24 on July 1, 2015, whichever is later. However, for an allocation area
25 identified as the Consolidated Allocation Area in the report submitted
26 in 2013 to the fiscal body under section 36.3 of this chapter, the
27 expiration date of any allocation provisions for the allocation area is
28 January 1, 2051. A declaratory resolution or an amendment that
29 establishes an allocation provision after June 30, 1995, must specify an
30 expiration date for the allocation provision. For an allocation area
31 established before July 1, 2008, the expiration date may not be more
32 than thirty (30) years after the date on which the allocation provision
33 is established. For an allocation area established after June 30, 2008,
34 the expiration date may not be more than twenty-five (25) years after
35 the date on which the first obligation was incurred to pay principal and
36 interest on bonds or lease rentals on leases payable from tax increment
37 revenues. However, with respect to bonds or other obligations that were
38 issued before July 1, 2008, if any of the bonds or other obligations that
39 were scheduled when issued to mature before the specified expiration
40 date and that are payable only from allocated tax proceeds with respect
41 to the allocation area remain outstanding as of the expiration date, the
42 allocation provision does not expire until all of the bonds or other
EH 1210—LS 6805/DI 134

296
1 obligations are no longer outstanding. The allocation provision may
2 apply to all or part of the redevelopment project area. The allocation
3 provision must require that any property taxes subsequently levied by
4 or for the benefit of any public body entitled to a distribution of
5 property taxes on taxable property in the allocation area be allocated
6 and distributed as follows:
7 (1) Except as otherwise provided in this section, the proceeds of
8 the taxes attributable to the lesser of:
9 (A) the assessed value of the property for the assessment date
10 with respect to which the allocation and distribution is made;
11 or
12 (B) the base assessed value;
13 shall be allocated to and, when collected, paid into the funds of
14 the respective taxing units.
15 (2) The excess of the proceeds of the property taxes imposed for
16 the assessment date with respect to which the allocation and
17 distribution is made that are attributable to taxes imposed after
18 being approved by the voters in a referendum or local public
19 question conducted after April 30, 2010, not otherwise included
20 in subdivision (1) shall be allocated to and, when collected, paid
21 into the funds of the taxing unit for which the referendum or local
22 public question was conducted.
23 (3) Except as otherwise provided in this section, property tax
24 proceeds in excess of those described in subdivisions (1) and (2)
25 shall be allocated to the redevelopment district and, when
26 collected, paid into a special fund for that allocation area that may
27 be used by the redevelopment district only to do one (1) or more
28 of the following:
29 (A) Pay the principal of and interest on any obligations
30 payable solely from allocated tax proceeds that are incurred by
31 the redevelopment district for the purpose of financing or
32 refinancing the redevelopment of that allocation area.
33 (B) Establish, augment, or restore the debt service reserve for
34 bonds payable solely or in part from allocated tax proceeds in
35 that allocation area.
36 (C) Pay the principal of and interest on bonds payable from
37 allocated tax proceeds in that allocation area and from the
38 special tax levied under section 19 of this chapter.
39 (D) Pay the principal of and interest on bonds issued by the
40 consolidated city to pay for local public improvements that are
41 physically located in or physically connected to that allocation
42 area.

1 (E) Pay premiums on the redemption before maturity of bonds

6 chapter.
7 (G) Reimburse the consolidated city for expenditures for local

1 subdivision.

35 the amount, if any, of excess assessed value determined by the
36 commission. The commission may not authorize an allocation
37 to the respective taxing units under this subdivision if to do so

39 in subdivision (3). If a commission fails to provide the

299
1 of allocated tax proceeds under this section to the
2 respective taxing units. However, if the commission notifies

6 without all or part of the allocated tax proceeds attributed
7 to the assessed value that has been allocated to the
8 respective taxing units, then the county auditor may not

10 allocation area that is used to calculate the allocation and
11 distribution of allocated tax proceeds under this section to
12 the respective taxing units.

35 (2) the base assessed value.

1 date after the petition.

10 (g) If any part of the allocation area is located in an enterprise zone
11 created under IC 5-28-15, the unit that designated the allocation area

19 (b)(1) and (b)(2) from property located in the enterprise zone that
20 exceeds the amount sufficient for the purposes specified in subsection
21 (b)(3) for the year. A unit that has no obligations, bonds, or leases
22 payable from allocated tax proceeds under subsection (b)(3) shall
23 establish a special zone fund and deposit all the property tax proceeds
24 in excess of those described in subsection (b)(1) and (b)(2) in the fund
